mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth and expanding consumer credit indicate strengthening aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating policy rate hikes compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate and signaling future economic slowdown, producing negative forward returns when tightening exceeds the threshold.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market tightening, which historically precedes wage-driven consumption booms and equity rallies within one week.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative SPX returns in > 60% of observations across any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion signals robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and equity valuations during normal business cycles.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero correlation with 20d SPX returns across any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts reflects weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, which transmits to broader economic activity and depresses equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines do not predict negative SPX returns in > 50% of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant policy rates indicates real rate compression, which erodes corporate profit margins and forces eventual aggressive tightening, negatively impacting equity returns.
failure_condition: Inflation-rate divergence shows no predictive power for negative equity returns over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth serves as a high-frequency leading indicator of economic momentum, directly translating into positive short-term equity market reactions as investors price in near-term earnings stability.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no directional predictability for 5-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 3.5% alongside robust housing construction, the economy exhibits synchronized demand strength that historically sustains equity outperformance over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Synchronized growth signals fail to produce positive SPX returns in > 55% of instances.
